title: Daemon Operations, Upgrades, Recovery, Cleanup, Orchestration Boundary, and Capston… · Delivery Case: Docker Compose Orchestration Boundary
summary: An operations board must approve a production container system through daemon upgrade, disk pressure, backup recovery, credential rotation, and migration pressure. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker compose orchestration boundary increment in production Docker operations and release defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State when Compose is sufficient and when scheduling, replicas, rolling updates, secrets, policy, load balancing, self-healing, or multi-host needs another platform.
Attribute disk use to images, build cache, writable layers, logs, volumes, and snapshots; preserve owners and backups before scoped cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker Compose Orchestration Boundary · Theory Docker Compose Orchestration Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory board reviews a digest-pinned multi-service release with an untested rollback, stale backup, daemon upgrade, capacity uncertainty, and unclear ownership. Defend rollout, recovery, compatibility, and residual limits. Competency probe: State when Compose is sufficient and when scheduling, replicas, rolling updates, secrets, policy, load balancing, self-healing, or multi-host needs another platform.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Compose Orchestration Boundary contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name client, daemon, builder, image, container, host-kernel, storage, network, registry, and supply-chain boundaries; predict state and authority; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, cleanup, and live-environment transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example board reviews a digest-pinned multi-service release with an untested rollback, stale backup, daemon upgrade, capacity uncertainty, and unclear ownership. Defend rollout, recovery, compatibility, and residual limits. Competency probe: State when Compose is sufficient and when scheduling, replicas, rolling updates, secrets, policy, load balancing, self-healing, or multi-host needs another platform. A defensible solution applies Docker Compose Orchestration Boundary, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to production Docker operations and release defense.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-production-operations-defense-5
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-production-operations-defense-5
    artifact: production-defense.yaml
    decision: worked docker production operations defense 5
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: ephemeral development host with constrained disk and memory
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      release: digest-pinned
      rollout: health-and-observation-gated
      rollback: rehearsed-previous-digest
      backup-restore: timed-and-proven
      upgrade: compatibility-canary
      capacity: measured-limits
      handoff: accessible-runbook
    verify: docker compose config --quiet && docker buildx imagetools inspect candidate@sha256:release
    failure: Reject latest-tag rollout, untested rollback, backup without restore, blind daemon upgrade, or a container boundary claimed as orchestrator durability.
    repair: Defend the operating model with immutable identity, canary signals, failure drills, recovery objectives, capacity evidence, ownership, and residual risks.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A production Compose file automatically provides the same scheduling and availability model as Kubernetes. This fails the Docker Compose Orchestration Boundary contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
